title: containerized: failed to connect to syslog: Initialization
body:
podman-in-container on [f38](https://api.cirrus-ci.com/v1/artifact/task/4969105690722304/html/int-podman-fedora-38-root-container-boltdb.log.html) pseudorandomly (i.e. flakily) emits the following warning:
```
failed to connect to syslog: Initialization
```
I do not see it on [containerized f37](https://api.cirrus-ci.com/v1/artifact/task/6095005597564928/html/int-podman-fedora-37-root-container-boltdb.log.html)

This is blocking #19802

comments.body:
@flouthoc can you take a look at this one?

I suspect the log to come from aadvark-dns: https://github.com/containers/aardvark-dns/blob/c9cbf7d5146c34e51c00b2bc190f41a011d5e3ea/src/main.rs#L63
Error is coming from the `syslog` crate itself when it tries to connect to local syslog using unix socket, let me try reproducing this, maybe we need a retry logic here. I'll check this.
@flouthoc did you find time to look into it?
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
Friendly ping.  Something for bug week next week?

The question is what should we do instead? Of course it is easy to just not log this error at all but loosing all logs on systems without syslog seems undesirable to me.
Unfortunately we cannot keep logging to stderr because aardvark-dns lifetime can be much longer then the command which spawned it meaning if you close your terminal aardvark-dns would die because of SIGPIPE.
But we already have this problem right now so I guess we ignore that for now and maybe only log this error when debug logs are requested like we do in podman
https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/a1982c043dc77f76d013ff24509110ef93f4233e/cmd/podman/syslog_common.go#L19-L21
I created https://github.com/containers/aardvark-dns/pull/402 to remove this line
